# services.keyd

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.keyd`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.keyd.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable keyd, a key remapping daemon. |
| `services.keyd.keyboards` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Configuration for one or more device IDs. Corresponding files in the /etc/keyd/ directory are created according to the name of the keys (like `default` or `externalKeyboard`). |
| `services.keyd.keyboards.<name>.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with "\n"` | Extra configuration that is appended to the end of the file. **Do not** write `ids` section here, use a separate option for it. You can use this option to define compound layers that must always be defined after the layer they are comprised. |
| `services.keyd.keyboards.<name>.ids` | `list of string` | Device identifiers, as shown by {manpage}`keyd(1)`. |
| `services.keyd.keyboards.<name>.settings` | `attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))` | Configuration, except `ids` section, that is written to {file}`/etc/keyd/<keyboard>.conf`. Appropriate names can be used to write non-alpha keys, for example "equal" instead of "=" sign (see <https://github.com/NixOS/nixpkgs/issues/236622>). See <https://github.com/rvaiya/keyd> how to configure. |
| `services.keyd.package` | `package` | The keyd package to use. |
